  <title>OpenClaw implements partial and progress modes for Telegram tool streaming visualization</title>
  <subtitle>Two distinct approaches balance tool visibility with streaming smoothness through editable status drafts and debouncing</subtitle>
  <facts>

  <narrative>OpenClaw's Telegram streaming implementation provides two configurable modes for handling tool progress visualization. Partial mode streams tool-progress updates directly into an editable preview message alongside thinking and reply text, using the same preview path but maintaining visual separation through layout. Progress mode takes a different approach by maintaining a dedicated status preview that shows tool activity independently of answer content - it materializes the status label when answer streaming begins but no tool line is available, then clears the draft upon completion while delivering the final answer through normal message delivery. Both modes implement debouncing to prevent notification spam while ensuring active runs don't stay visually silent, materializing previews after a bounded delay. This dual-mode architecture offers flexibility: partial mode provides integrated visibility of tools and answers in one preview, while progress mode isolates mutable tool status from answer streaming to avoid animation conflicts. Configuration is controlled via streaming.preview.toolProgress and streaming.preview.commandText settings.</narrative>
